title: State of the Feed — Week of June 22–28, 2026
content_html:
During the week of June 22–28, 2026, the aggregator processed 8 runs across all configured feeds, fetching an average of 1122 articles per run and delivering an average of 39 quality articles per run. The weekly output totaled 312 quality articles across nine content categories. News dominated the feed at 160 articles, followed by wellness at 57 articles and AI-tech at 43 articles. Local, climate, homelab, scifi, and science content comprised the remaining 90 articles combined, with science representing minimal volume at a single article for the week.

The dimensional quality-relevance-local scoring model maintained its configured weighting of 0.25 for quality, 0.55 for relevance, and 0.20 for local signals. Two new feeds were added during the period: LoRaMeshDevices and Daniele Messi—Writing, 3D Printing blog. All existing feeds operated without errors. No baseline pipeline efficiency data is currently available for comparison, preventing assessment of the noise-to-signal ratio against established benchmarks. Feed discovery reporting was not generated for this cycle.
